summary:
Great! All tests are now passing. We've successfully fixed the issue by updating the SQL queries in the main.go file to use the correct column name "order_status" instead of the old column name "status".

Here's a summary of what we did:

1. We identified that the issue was in the main.go file where the SQL queries were using the old column name "status" instead of the new column name "order_status".
2. We updated the SQL queries in the createOrder and getOrder functions to use the correct column name.
3. We built the Go application with the updated code.
4. We ran the test script and verified that all tests are now passing.

The fix was straightforward - we just needed to update the SQL queries to match the database schema after the migration. This is a common issue when dealing with database migrations, and it's important to ensure that the application code is updated to match the schema changes.
